editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Mr. Frederick Wears applying to Dublin Castle for a job as a messenger or a similar position. Wears claims to have five years experience and enjoys the traveling.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3925

correspAction.placeName: 7 Priors Buildings, Harrogate Hill, Darlington, UK

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
28/2/16 7 Priors Buildings
Harrogate Hill
Darlington Dear Sir, Thinking their is no
harm I write you enquiring
as to whether you could offer
me employment of some
description. I am twenty-
seven years of age & I am
married with no other
dependants but my wife &
mother & at the present
time I am not engaged
on munition but as a
labourer & considering the
fact that I left the Post
Office Service with a good
character after five years
temporary service as
telegraph messenger &
auxiliary postman. I am
very desirious of obtaining
a situation as messenger
either at the castle or a
railway company or some
position of trust but preferably
a messengers position as
there would be a deal
of travelling etc. I would
would also like to state
that I have offered & have
been rejected quite recently
for the army & could produce
a rejection certificate if
necessary & if your
excellency could help me
in my request I can
assure you that I would
do my utmost to merit
your confidence
& to hear favourably from
you. I remain Sir
Yours Respectfully Fredrick Wears
